title: Kidney Gross Anatomy
buzzwords: Paired retroperitoneal bean-shaped filtering organs
Receives roughly a quarter of cardiac output
Sits behind peritoneum from T12 to L3
Right one sits lower because of liver
Cortex, medulla, pyramids drain to renal pelvis
Wrapped by capsule, perirenal fat, and Gerota fascia
Hilum transmits renal artery, vein, and ureter
Left renal vein crosses under the SMA (nutcracker)

title: Ureter Anatomy
buzzwords: Twenty-five centimeter retroperitoneal muscular tube
Smooth muscle peristalsis propels fluid downstream
Crosses anterior to psoas and common iliacs
Segmental blood supply from many adjacent arteries
Three narrowings predispose to stone impaction
Colicky flank-to-groin pain with hematuria
In females passes under the uterine artery
Narrows at UPJ, pelvic brim, and UVJ

title: Urinary Bladder Anatomy
buzzwords: Hollow muscular pelvic reservoir behind pubic symphysis
Has apex, body, fundus, and neck
Oblique entry of inflow tubes prevents reflux
Wall smooth muscle contracts to expel contents
Superior and inferior vesical arteries supply it
Sympathetics store, parasympathetics (S2-S4) void
Detrusor contraction empties via the internal urethral orifice
Trigone bounded by two ureteric orifices and urethra

title: Urethra Anatomy
buzzwords: Terminal conduit from bladder to outside
Two sphincters regulate outflow
Internal sphincter involuntary; external sphincter voluntary
Female version short, predisposes to UTIs
Male version about 18-20 cm, three segments
Membranous portion vulnerable to catheterization rupture
Prostatic, membranous, and spongy male divisions
Pudendal nerve controls external sphincter voluntarily
